title: [cross-impl] Trickle reset condition inconsistent (Python != vs Rust/C >)
description: After commit 5e72e6b03 aligned C (lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/trickle.c:114) and Rust (rust/lichen-rpl/src/trickle.rs:178) reset() to `if stopped or interval > imin`, Python (python/src/lichen/rpl/trickle.py:90) still uses `if _generation==0 or interval != imin`. RFC 6206 rule 6 says reset if I > Imin. This can cause divergent behavior on edge cases (interval < imin, initial state, generation proxy vs explicit state). Matches comments in rpl_trickle.h:156 but not Python reference impl. Test vectors and sim tests may not catch interop timing diffs. See lichen_rpl_dodag and trickle tests.
